summary: Iter-9 code-review polish regression. ALL GREEN. Backend pytest suite 25/25 pass (same /app/backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter7_widget.py — covers widget.js, /api/embed/<slug> public + internal, theme=light, avg_pct cap, auth/me admin, register+me, optimize public, billing plans/monthly/annual checkout, reports/savings.pdf, dashboard/overview, admin/overview RBAC ok+forbidden, share/savings create+get). Frontend smoke confirmed end-to-end: Landing (hero/calculator/counters), Login (QA user alreadyherellc@gmail.com), Dashboard (quota-meter, kpi-grid, chart-tokens render — quotaColor helper applied 'text-[rgb(var(--tf-success))]' on <80% branch), Billing (current-plan-card, cycle-toggle Monthly→Annual, billing-plan-pro), Keys, Logs, Playground, Pricing, Docs all load without runtime errors. Static code review of the four refactor sites confirms (1) Landing.jsx:58 console.warn correctly gated behind NODE_ENV !== 'production'; (2) auth.jsx wraps context value in useMemo and login/register/logout/fetchMe in useCallback with stable empty deps — signatures + return shape ({ok, error?}) unchanged; (3) Dashboard.jsx exports CHART_TOOLTIP_STYLE + CHART_TOOLTIP_LABEL_STYLE at module scope (passed to both LineChart Tooltip and BarChart Tooltip — fixes the per-render remount); (4) quotaColor/quotaBarColor in Dashboard.jsx and usageBarColor in Billing.jsx implement identical >=100→error / >=80→warning / else→success mapping as the prior inline nested ternary. Pure refactor, zero behavior change confirmed.
critical_code_review_comments: auth.jsx — useCallback deps [] for login/register/logout are correct because setToken/setUser/client/formatApiErrorDetail are module-scoped or stable setters; no stale-closure risk. Memoization is sound.

Dashboard.jsx — CHART_TOOLTIP_STYLE + CHART_TOOLTIP_LABEL_STYLE moved to module scope is the correct fix; Recharts <Tooltip> now receives stable refs across renders and will not remount its portal layer.

quotaColor/quotaBarColor (Dashboard) and usageBarColor (Billing) are duplicated — three nearly-identical 3-branch helpers. Optional consolidation: extract one shared helper to /app/frontend/src/lib/quota.js (e.g., `usageColor(pct, kind: 'text'|'bg')`) to remove the duplication. Not blocking.
Landing.jsx — `console.warn` gating is correct; consider extending the same pattern to other dev-only warnings if any are added later (currently only the public-stats fetch warns).
No new files >700 lines; no ObjectId leaks; all .env access patterns preserved; data-testid coverage intact across changed pages.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (8 pages smoke-verified, quota color helper resolved class verified in DOM, billing cycle toggle works)

test_credentials: Admin admin@tokenforge.io / ForgeAdmin!2026 + QA alreadyherellc@gmail.com / ForgeQA!2026 (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md)
seed_data_creation: None — reused existing accounts. Backend tests use ephemeral TEST_iter7_*@example.com throwaway users.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter-9 was a pure refactor: console.warn gating in Landing.jsx, useMemo/useCallback in auth.jsx, module-scope Recharts tooltip style consts in Dashboard.jsx, and quotaColor/quotaBarColor/usageBarColor helpers replacing nested ternaries in Dashboard.jsx + Billing.jsx. No new endpoints, no new data-testids, no behavior changes. Existing test suite (/app/backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter7_widget.py — 25 tests) covers full regression; rerun with `pytest backend/tests/test_tokenforge_iter7_widget.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_iterN.xml`. The QA user has low monthly usage so quota color resolves to tf-success on Dashboard; to exercise the warning/error branches in UI, an admin endpoint or DB tweak would be needed to bump the test user's tokens_used past 80%/100% of monthly_quota — not done this iter since the helper logic is trivially verifiable by code inspection and the success branch was confirmed live.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no issues found. Refactor verified to be a no-op via (a) all 25 prior backend regression tests still pass; (b) 8 frontend pages render and interact correctly; (c) static review confirms identical mapping in extracted helpers vs prior inline nested ternaries; (d) auth.jsx useCallback deps are empty and safe since all captured refs are module-scoped or stable React setters.
